response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a skilled individual contributor with a broad technical background spanning distributed systems, full-stack development, and early agentic-AI tooling, but the resume shows no evidence of executive or people-management experience at any scale. The CTO role requires demonstrated leadership scaling engineering organizations from 10 to 30+ people, ownership of multi-agent AI systems in production, and deep fintech infrastructure expertise — none of which are present. Her AI involvement at re:cinq began in April 2025 and is described at a contributor level, not an architectural or strategic one. The role-to-candidate gap is fundamental rather than addressable through coaching.
strengths: Hands-on distributed-systems and high-throughput microservices experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O)
Early practical exposure to agentic workflows and MCP tooling at re:cinq, showing awareness of the emerging paradigm
Consistent track record of full-stack ownership across varied domains and tech stacks over nearly 20 years
concerns: Zero evidence of executive or people-management experience — no team-lead, director, VP, or CTO title; no mention of hiring, performance management, or org design
AI and agentic-systems experience is shallow and very recent (April 2025), with no production ownership of multi-agent architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or AI reliability infrastructure

No meaningful fintech-infrastructure depth — brief stint as an IC developer at RIDE Capital does not translate to credit-system architecture, payment flows, or real-time risk modeling at a fintech platform
